page: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 40 MB of L3 cache, 32 KB of L1d cache, and 0.25 MB of L2 cache. The system is equipped with 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 28.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 459 GB drive, and there are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance tradeoffs. Single-core CPU performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% tier. Multi-core performance is also constrained, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 3433.0 and a passmark CPU score of 6589.06, both placing in the bottom 25%. However, memory subsystem performance is mixed; while large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weak to poor, cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaches an average tier at 454.13 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations at 6.85 million operations per second also perform at an average level, alongside average multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the GS4-8 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense computational throughput. The high RAM density offsets the lower CPU performance for specific memory-resident tasks. Tradeoffs include weak cryptographic and compression speeds, as well as low static web serving throughput. This server is best suited for memory-intensive database operations, caching layers such as Redis, and lightweight text or prompt processing tasks that fit entirely within its 224.0 GB memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores, 8 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 224.0 GB of RAM, and 459 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmarks indicate weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores in the lower percentiles. However, it maintains average performance in database operations, Redis SET operations at 6.85 million operations per second, and cached small-block memory bandwidth. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 28 GB per core makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, compute-light workloads. It is best suited for memory-resident databases, caching layers, and basic text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor, 8 vCPUs, 224 GB of RAM, and 459 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB of RAM, and 459 GB of local storage for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-heavy virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-core density and local storage for memory-resident databases and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs.
High memory density of 224.0 GB RAM, providing 28.0 GB per core.
Includes 459 GB of local storage with no bundled GPU.
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET operations.
Weak single-core and multi-core computational benchmark results.
Best suited for memory-resident databases, caching layers, and lightweight text processing.
